title: Razorpay service module — Orders API, signature verify, credit consumption
acceptance_criteria: File exports a named function 'initRazorpay()' that reads RAZORPAY_KEY_ID and RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET from env; throws a descriptive error if called when PAYMENT_ENABLED is false or keys are missing; never called at module load time (lazy init pattern)

Exports 'createOrder(params: { amountPaise: number; currency: string; receipt: string; notes?: Record<string,string> }): Promise<{ orderId: string; amount: number; currency: string }>'. Uses Razorpay REST API (fetch or razorpay npm package). Returns typed result.

Exports 'verifyPaymentSignature(orderId: string, paymentId: string, signature: string): boolean'. Computes HMAC-SHA256 of '${orderId}|${paymentId}' using RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET. Returns boolean, never throws on bad input.

Exports 'verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody: Buffer, headerSignature: string): boolean'. Computes HMAC-SHA256 of rawBody using RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET. CRITICAL: rawBody must be a Buffer — function must not accept string to prevent accidental re-serialization. Returns boolean, never throws.

Exports 'consumeCredit(db: Pool, feature: string, amount?: number): Promise<{ success: boolean; remainingBalance: number }>'. Atomically: reads current SUM(credits_delta) from credit_transactions, checks balance >= amount (default 1), if sufficient inserts a row with credits_delta = -(amount), returns success=true and new balance. If insufficient, returns success=false with current balance. Uses a DB transaction to prevent race conditions.
Exports 'getCreditBalance(db: Pool): Promise<number>'. Returns current SUM(credits_delta) or 0 if no rows.
All exports are named (no default export)
No secrets are logged or included in error messages
output_format: src/payment/razorpay.ts plus plain-English explanation of the HMAC raw-body requirement and why consumeCredit uses a DB transaction
